- Three viewer source files (elapsed-time.ts, route-state.ts, task-audit.ts) are listed as entry points for this zone but belong in the web-viewer zone per developer-provided hints — zone membership correction is needed to restore accurate cohesion and coupling metrics.
- Coupling of 0.67 exceeds the healthy threshold and is an artifact of the misclassified viewer files; resolving zone membership will likely bring coupling back into range without any code changes.
- build.js and dev.js are correctly identified as entrypoints rather than static config — this distinction matters for treeshaking and bundler entry resolution in the esbuild pipeline.
- This zone's anomalous metrics are a calibration artifact, but the correct zone boundary after reassigning viewer files would be: build.js, dev.js, package.json, tsconfig.json — a pure build-config cluster with no cross-zone coupling, cohesion unmeasurable due to no JS import edges.
- Once viewer files are reassigned, this zone becomes a zero-import config cluster similar to rex-runtime-state and project-documentation — consider explicitly tagging it as a build-config zone in sourcevision hints so cohesion/coupling metrics are interpreted correctly rather than triggering false architectural warnings.
- build.js and dev.js are JavaScript (not TypeScript) entry points that invoke the esbuild pipeline — any configuration object passed to esbuild is untyped, so invalid build options fail at runtime rather than typecheck. A TypeScript-native build config (e.g., build.ts executed via tsx) would catch option-type errors at development time.
- esbuild configuration in build.js/dev.js is untyped JavaScript — invalid option names or value types silently fail at build time with no typecheck safety net. Migrating to tsx-executed TypeScript build scripts would close this gap.
